text.title: Embeddings Production Ops — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `embeddings-production-ops` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: An SLO (latency_p95, success_rate) is documented and tracked. Without SLO, ops is reactive.
rule.rationale: SLO-less ops fixes problems only after users complain; SLO-driven ops fixes them before.

rule.statement: Embedding API calls use exponential backoff + jitter on 429 / 5xx. Bare retries rejected.
rule.rationale: Bare retries on 429 escalate the rate-limit; backoff + jitter is the only friendly approach.

rule.statement: Every embedding call records (tenant / use case, token count, $cost). Anonymous spend forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, cost spikes can't be traced; cross-charging breaks.

rule.statement: Provider outage triggers degraded mode (cached embeddings, fallback model). Hard failure is rejected.
rule.rationale: Outages happen; without fallback the whole feature breaks.

rule.statement: Model swap is a documented playbook with dual-index window. In-place model swap forbidden.
rule.rationale: In-place swap mixes embeddings from two models in the same index → broken cosine distances → broken retrieval.
